channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Rajasthan Student Reports Ear Injury Allegedly from JBL Earbud, Company Comments
item.description: A student from Rajasthan reported an alarming incident where a JBL earbud allegedly burst while in use inside his ear, causing discomfort and concern. The student shared the incident online, drawing a...

item.title: Alcaraz defeats Vacherot to set up Monte Carlo final against Sinner
item.description: Carlos Alcaraz, the defending champion and top seed, defeated Valentin Vacherot 6-4, 6-4 to end the Monegasque’s impressive run at the Monte Carlo Masters. Vacherot, who had previously made a breakthr...

item.title: Bangladeshi spiritual leader killed in mob violence amid rising religious tensions
item.description: A self-proclaimed spiritual leader, Shamim Reza Jahangir, was brutally beaten to death by an angry mob in Bangladesh’s Kushtia district on Saturday. The incident occurred after an old video resurfaced...

item.title: Western Australia considers establishing state-controlled fuel reserves
item.description: The Western Australian government has indicated plans to establish its own strategic fuel reserve in response to recent diesel shortages affecting the agriculture sector, which have been linked to ong...

item.title: Namit Arora Responds on Anachronism and Presentism in Historical Interpretation
item.description: In this piece, Namit Arora, co-author of 'Speaking of History', responds to reflections by Manash Firaq Bhattacharjee published in Scroll. Arora addresses the challenges of anachronism and presentism ...

item.title: Operation Safe Corridor: Nigeria’s Programme to Rehabilitate and Reintegrate Low-Risk Former Insurgents
item.description: Operation Safe Corridor na one non-kinetic programme wey Nigeria government launch since 2016 to help deradicalise and reintegrate former Boko Haram members wey dem classify as low-risk. According to ...

item.title: Pakistan and Egypt to launch Borrowers’ Platform amid rising debt challenges
item.description: ISLAMABAD: Pakistan and Egypt are set to co-lead the launch of the 'Borrowers’ Platform' on April 15 in Washington, during the IMF-World Bank Spring meetings, at the United Nations Foundation. This in...

item.title: President Marcos and First Lady Liza have a quiet dinner amid health discussions
item.description: On April 11, 2026, President Ferdinand 'Bongbong' Marcos Jr. and First Lady Liza Araneta Marcos enjoyed a quiet dinner together at Finestra Restaurant in Solaire Resort, Parañaque. Both shared photos ...

item.title: Tyson Fury discusses wife's miscarriage during Usyk fight and their emotional journey
item.description: Tyson Fury has opened up about the heart-wrenching experience of learning that his wife, Paris Fury, suffered a miscarriage while he was preparing for his 2024 fight against Oleksandr Usyk. The couple...

item.title: Structural Inequalities Drive African Health Worker Migration to Wealthier Nations
item.description: Africa is facing a major shortage of health workers, with estimates suggesting a deficit of five to six million by 2030. This shortage affects the delivery of basic health services and the ability to ...

item.title: Royal Canadian Navy personnel in Regina recognized for service and leadership at HMCS Queen ceremony
item.description: A recognition ceremony was recently held at HMCS Queen in Regina to celebrate the achievements and dedication of local members of the Royal Canadian Navy. The event highlighted the service, leadership...

item.title: NASA Commander Opens Up About Preparing Family Before Artemis II Lunar Flyby
item.description: Commander Reid Wiseman, the 50-year-old astronaut who led NASA’s Artemis II mission, shared an emotional story about preparing his teenage daughters for the risks of space travel. Knowing that the mis...

item.title: Investor Complacency Persists Despite Global Crises
item.description: Despite multiple global crises over the past few years, including the COVID-19 pandemic, Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, tariff conflicts, and recent tensions in the Middle East, financial markets have ...

item.title: KALINGA Act aims to stabilize fuel prices and support vulnerable sectors
item.description: House Speaker Faustino 'Bojie' Dy III and House Majority Leader Ferdinand Alexander 'Sandro' Marcos have filed House Bill No. 8834, known as the KALINGA Act, which seeks to establish a fast and automa...

item.title: Pakistan’s Diplomatic Role and Prospects for Pak-Iran Relations
item.description: The recent US-Iran negotiations hosted by Pakistan mark a potentially important shift in regional geopolitics. Pakistan has acted as a mediator, attempting to bridge the gap between long-time adversar...

item.title: Uche Ogbodo Defend Bambam, Blame Husband for Marriage Breakup
item.description: Nollywood actress Uche Ogbodo don come out to defend her colleague Bambam wey be former Big Brother Naija star, after plenty people begin dey talk say the lady leave her marriage because she do body s...

item.title: Eric Swalwell faces allegations over campaign-funded payments to nanny without work authorization
item.description: Rep. Eric Swalwell, D-Calif., currently running for governor, faces allegations regarding the employment of his live-in nanny, Amanda Barbosa. Complaints filed with the Department of Labor and the Dep...

item.title: Veteran playback singer Asha Bhosle dies at 92 after cardiac arrest in Mumbai hospital
item.description: Veteran Indian playback singer Asha Bhosle, one of the most iconic voices of Hindi cinema, passed away on Sunday, April 12, 2026, at the age of 92 in Mumbai. She had been admitted to the Breach Candy ...

item.title: Diokno says Madriaga’s testimony crucial in VP Sara Duterte impeachment hearings
item.description: House Deputy Minority Leader Chel Diokno of Akbayan party-list said the House Committee on Justice expects Ramil Madriaga, a confessed bagman of Vice President Sara Duterte, to appear at the impeachme...

item.title: US-Iran Peace Talks Collapse Amid Strait of Hormuz Tensions
item.description: Peace negotiations between the United States and Iran, held in Islamabad and brokered by Pakistan, concluded without an agreement after 21 hours of talks. US Vice President JD Vance highlighted Iran's...
